name: copy
context: Transfer files over SSH with scp or sftp, including recursive copies, batch mode, and bastions
info: This skill covers the file-transfer surface of OpenSSH. `scp` is the quickest path for ad hoc uploads and downloads and now uses the SFTP protocol by default, while `sftp` adds interactive and batch workflows for repeatable transfers. Agents use this skill to push deploy artifacts, pull logs and backups, move files through bastions, preserve timestamps, and run transfer jobs without interactive prompts.
body:
# File Transfer with scp and sftp

Use [[ssh/copy]] when the job is moving bytes, not opening a shell. `scp` is the simplest wrapper for file copy. `sftp` is better when you need repeatable batch commands, partial-transfer control, or an interactive remote file browser.

## Quick Upload and Download

```bash
scp dist/app.tar.gz ops@host.example.com:/srv/releases/
scp ops@host.example.com:/var/log/api.log ./api.log
scp -r public/ ops@host.example.com:/srv/www/
```

`scp` uses the SFTP protocol over an SSH connection by default. `-O` forces the legacy SCP protocol only when compatibility requires it.

## sftp Batch Mode

```bash
printf '%s
' 'put -p build.zip /srv/build.zip' 'get -p /var/log/app.log ./app.log' | sftp -b - ops@host.example.com
```

Batch files are useful when an agent needs deterministic uploads and downloads that abort on failure.

## Jump Hosts and Limits

```bash
scp -J bastion.example.com backup.sql ops@db.internal.example.com:/tmp/
sftp -J bastion.example.com -l 2048 ops@db.internal.example.com
```

Both `scp` and `sftp` inherit bastions, identities, and multiplexing from [[ssh/config]].

sections.title: scp versus sftp
sections.body: `scp` is fast and terse for one-shot upload or download workflows. `sftp` is better when you need a command loop, batch files, resumed transfers, or remote file browsing. `scp` uses the SFTP protocol by default; pass `-O` only for legacy SCP compatibility. See [[ssh/connect]] for the shared transport layer.

sections.title: Uploads, downloads, and recursive copy
sections.body: Use `scp local remote:/path` for uploads and `scp remote:/path local` for downloads. Add `-r` to copy directories recursively and `-p` to preserve timestamps and modes. In `sftp`, the equivalent commands are `put`, `get`, `put -R`, and `get -R` with optional `-p` preservation.

sections.title: Batch transfers
sections.body: `sftp -b batchfile host` runs a deterministic list of transfer commands and aborts on command failure unless you prefix a line with `-`. Use batch mode together with key-based auth and `BatchMode yes` from [[ssh/connect]] or [[ssh/config]] for unattended artifact movement.

links.context: scp and sftp reuse the same SSH auth and host verification model
Store bastions, identities, and multiplexing for transfer commands
Use agent-backed keys for repeated transfers
Debug transfer failures with scp -v or sftp -vvv
Combine SSH transfer with local file operations

related.note: Transfers inherit the same connection, auth, and host-key rules as ssh
Persistent host aliases and ProxyJump apply to both scp and sftp
Use port forwards when the remote service is not file-oriented

scripts.name: Transfer files over SSH
scripts.description: Upload or download files and directories using scp or sftp

SSH_PROXY_JUMP.description: Jump host or chain passed to -J

SSH_LOCAL_PATH.description: Local path to upload or local destination for download

SSH_REMOTE_PATH.description: Remote path to upload to or download from

SSH_BATCH_FILE.description: sftp batch file path; bypasses generated get/put commands

SSH_RECURSIVE.description: Set to true for recursive directory copy

SSH_PRESERVE_TIMES.description: Set to true to preserve timestamps and modes

SSH_LEGACY_SCP.description: Set to true to force scp -O

SSH_EXTRA_ARGS.description: Extra tool arguments, one per line

exitCode.description: Exit code from scp or sftp

depends.reason: scp and sftp rely on the same SSH auth and trust model
